source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit.Tests/DataFrame/PlottingPositionTests.cs (v2.0.0, ARRANGE2/PPLOT2/PLPOS rewrite, T12). Each case wraps the C# test's bare DataFrame scenario in a minimal Normal model (the model_estimation `data_frame` surface has no bare-DataFrame construct shape). The `Simulation` target is used (rather than MaximumLikelihood/MaximumAPosteriori) because these 1-2 observation scenarios are deliberately too small for the real C# NelderMead optimizer to reliably converge (`MaximumLikelihood.Estimate()` returns false for them against the real library) -- the `plotting_position`/`number_of_low_outliers`/`low_outlier_threshold` DataFrame surface is reachable under ANY file-level target (see fixtures/README.md's model_estimation section), so `Simulation` (which builds the model and takes one harmless seeded draw, with no fit requirement) is the right vehicle here; the `sample_size`/seed draw itself is not asserted.

assertions.note: C# Test_PlottingPositions_ValueBelowOwnThreshold_UsesCensoredBranch: explicit value below its OWN covering threshold is classified censored (ARRANGE2), not by a globally-sorted band.

C# Test_PlottingPositions_AggregateThresholdCounts_AffectRanks (4d/15d): three left-censored placeholders, one finite detection, one right-censored placeholder -- detection probability 2/(2+3); the finite detection ranks before the right-censored placeholder.

C# Test_PlottingPositions_OutsideThresholdWindow_IsDetected: an observation outside every threshold window is detected regardless of magnitude vs. an unrelated finite threshold (synthetic negative-infinity threshold, ARRANGE2).
